name: Conrad Janis
abstract: Conrad Janis (February 11, 1928 – March 1, 2022) was an American jazz trombonist and actor who starred in film and television during the Golden Age Era in the 1950s and 1960s, and continued acting right up until 2012. He played the role of Mindy McConnell's father Frederick in 53 episodes of Mork & Mindy, and appeared in many guest-starr…

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Lovett
about_quotes.evidence: I worked, for about a year and a half I made a living working with Conrad Janis... And I played with him for a while.

about_quotes.speaker: David Albert Francis
about_quotes.evidence: I played with them up there for 10 years. It was Conrad... I was with Conrad Janis, who had the group up there.

about_quotes.speaker: R.C.H. Smith
about_quotes.evidence: there was a band that put together with clarinetist Tom Sharpstein and Conrad Janis was the trombone player... our band won

about_quotes.speaker: Freddie Moore
about_quotes.evidence: started working with Conrad Janis. And that eventually led to some recordings of Conrad Janis and His Tailgate Jazz Band.

about_quotes.speaker: Big Chielf Moore
about_quotes.evidence: I got to play the Central Plaza with quite a number of other people like ... Conrad Janis was there, too.

about_quotes.speaker: Hentoff Nat
about_quotes.evidence: I didn’t hear – I don’t know what Conrad Janis really ever sounded like.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hentoff Nat — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
